title: Coespeletia spicata
intro: Coespeletia spicata, also known as chuchuhuasi, is a plant native to the Peruvian Amazon, belonging to the family Compositae and commonly used as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Coespeletia spicata include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been found to contribute to its medicinal properties. Coespeletia spicata is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support joint health and alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis and other musculoskeletal conditions.
taxonomy: Coespeletia spicata belongs to the family Compositae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Coespeletia. The genus Coespeletia is part of the tribe Mutisieae, which contains over 100 species of flowering plants. The Mutisieae tribe is classified under the subfamily Mutisioideae, a subfamily within the Compositae family. Coespeletia spicata is native to the Andean region of South America.
